questions.prompt: Definitive classification of many odontogenic jaw lesions still requires:

options.text: Histopathology correlated with clinical and imaging findings
Vitality testing as the sole diagnostic step

questions.prompt: A radicular cyst lining is classically described as:

options.text: Non-keratinized stratified squamous epithelium in an inflammatory setting
Parakeratinized epithelium with palisaded basal cells only
Malignant spindle cell sarcoma without epithelium

questions.prompt: Ameloblastoma histology is most associated with:

options.text: Odontogenic epithelial islands with peripheral columnar cells
Keratinizing surface epithelium of gingiva without invasion

questions.prompt: When imaging suggests an aggressive multilocular radiolucency, the teaching priority is to:

options.text: Plan biopsy and discuss differential including OKC and ameloblastoma
Assume benign mucocele and discharge without follow-up
Avoid tissue sampling because 3D models are sufficient
Treat every lesion with pulp extirpation only
